topic: Cepheid/SBF distance-redshift — verdict reclassification (Misleading -> Self-Contradicted) per EXP-295 (final WIN of the 5-WIN package)
change_summary: Analyst (EXP-295) recommends WIN-055 verdict Misleading -> Self-Contradicted under sub_rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' along with a tldr_verdict rewrite, completing the five-WIN reclassification package (047/048/052/054 already integrated under qid:318-321 c3/c4 reviews; this is the fifth and final). Verdict-review items defeat Step 8a batching gate by definition (criterion 2: verdict change recommended), so this is a singleton-only run.
our_argument_summary: WIN-055 currently sits at verdict 'Misleading' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:verdict). The current finding reads 'Cepheid/SBF distances via Magellanic parallax place galaxies at 10-100 Mpc with no cosmological assumption; dome has no aetheric pulsation or surface-brightness mechanism' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:finding). The detail_evidence already states verbatim: 'These measurements place galaxies at 10-100 Mpc (30-300 million light-years) -- orders of magnitude beyond the dome's firmament at 9,086 km. The dome model has no spatial framework for where these galaxies exist, no mechanism for cosmological redshift, and no equation relating distance to it' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence). That language meets the existing Self-Contradicted pattern (2) -- structural-coherence failures where the dome's own commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome's own terms -- already used for WIN-029, WIN-053, WIN-067, and now WIN-047/048/052/054 per the just-applied EXP-295 patches. The verdict label is being aligned with what the prose already argues; this is not a new framework reading.

endorsement.rationale: The SC reading is justified by the existing detail_evidence verbatim. Three independent stellar-physics rungs (Cepheids, TRGB, SBF) are anchored to Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax and Gaia DR3 (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence) -- they are framework-independent distance measurements that place galaxies at 10-100 Mpc. The dome's published spatial framework bounds the entire model at order 10^7 m (raw-text/05-model.txt: 'Semi-major axis (a): ~20,015 km'; 'H at pole (r=0): 8,537 km'; data/wins.json:WIN-029:verdict='Self-Contradicted' for the Schumann-derived 9,086 km firmament parameter). The mismatch is six orders of magnitude (10 Mpc ~ 3.1e20 m vs ~10^7 m). The dome does not contest the existence of galaxies in any public document; it commits to a closed spatial framework that mathematically excludes them. Citing galaxy-anchored distance measurements as confirmation while publishing parameters that foreclose galaxies on the dome's own terms is the SC pattern (2) structure exactly. Of the five EXP-295 candidates, WIN-055 is approximately tied with WIN-052 for cleanest existing-prose support: the '10-100 Mpc / orders of magnitude beyond' phrasing is verbatim, no analyst gloss required.
endorsement.pattern_match: WIN-055 fits SC pattern (2) cleanly: (a) the dome's commitments are explicit and propagate to WIN-029 (firmament_height parameter, already SC -- data/wins.json:WIN-029:verdict='Self-Contradicted'); (b) the contradiction is dome-internal in the relevant sense (the dome's published spatial framework cannot host the referents the WIN cites); (c) the dilemma framing is verbatim in the WIN's own prose ('The dome model has no spatial framework for where these galaxies exist'); (d) standard candles' framework-independence (Magellanic parallax + Gaia + TRGB convergence) means the defender cannot deflect to 'maybe the cosmological assumptions are wrong' -- the distances are anchored to direct geometric measurements. WIN-055 is the cleanest case AGAINST the 'silent on extragalactic ontology' counter-argument because the distance-ladder construction is geometric, not redshift-derived.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not present in current snapshot; tag carried forward from WIN-055.c2 -- c2 confirmed no domain for distance-ladder validation)
dome_code_analysis.note: WIN-055 code_analysis tags were validated in c2; this cycle is a verdict-label change, not a code-tag change. Tags below carry forward from c2 unchanged.
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated calculation. WIN-055 has monitoring=none. The 'prediction' is the empirical Cepheid/SBF distance scale from Riess et al. 2022 SH0ES (10.3847/2041-8213/ac5c5b), Blakeslee et al. 2021 (10.3847/1538-4357/abf81c), and Freedman et al. 2019 CCHP TRGB (10.3847/1538-4357/ab2f73) -- adopted wholesale; no dome-side derivation produces the relation.
data_sources_used: Riess+ 2022 SH0ES ApJL 934 L7 (Cepheid distance ladder)
Gaia DR3 parallax (geometric anchor, ~1% Milky Way Cepheid distances)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None at the code level (no automation). The detail_verdict_text (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_verdict_text) explicitly invokes the 'trivial-transcription test' (Part 3.1b) and notes the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call -- making even the dome's own 'timestamped before confirming data was pulled' claim unverifiable for this WIN.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2. WIN-055 has no domain in monitor.py; standard candles cannot be validated by the dome's automation pipeline. (data/wins.json:WIN-055:code_analysis.monitoring='none')
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward from c2. The Cepheid period-luminosity relation (Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax) and SBF surface-brightness ratios are textbook standard candles. The dome 'predicts' the standard distance ladder while providing no aetheric mechanism for stellar pulsation periods or surface-brightness variations across megaparsecs. This is relabeling: the values are textbook physics, the citation is dome.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward from c2. Cepheid distance ladder predates the dome by decades (Hubble 1929; Sandage 1958; modern HST Key Project 1990s; Riess+ 2022 SH0ES). SBF method introduced by Tonry & Schneider 1988. TRGB method by Lee, Freedman & Madore 1993. All decades before the dome's late-March-2026 import.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward from c2. Zero derivation chain from disc_radius=20015 km / firmament_height=9086 km / sun_altitude=5733 km / moon_altitude=2534 km to the Cepheid period-luminosity relation, the SBF magnitude scale, or the TRGB luminosity cutoff. The dome provides no aetheric formula for stellar pulsation or surface-brightness ratios.

holes_found.description: The proposed_subsection_html for Part 6.13 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json:proposed_subsection_html) contains a numerical error in the body: 'The firmament height function H(r) tops out at approximately 8,619 kilometers'. The dome's published H(r) formula is H(r) = 8,537 x exp(-r / 8,619) km (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt; raw-text/05-model.txt). The maximum occurs at r=0 and equals 8,537 km, NOT 8,619 km. The 8,619 figure is the EXPONENTIAL DECAY LENGTH in the formula, not a height. The TLDR of the same proposed section uses 9,086 km consistently (the WIN-029 V50.6 model parameterization, which is also the value used in this WIN-055 detail_evidence prose). The body's '8,619' figure is a third number that is neither the formula peak nor the parameterized value -- it is the decay scale. A defender will spot this immediately ('they cannot even copy our parameter correctly') and use it to attack the section's authority. This finding is on the cross-cutting Part 6.13 section and not on WIN-055's tldr_verdict patch itself, but the section is the rendered home of the WIN-055 SC sub_rationale's 'See Part 6.13' pointer, so a numerical error in the section weakens the audit-trail surface that justifies the reclassification.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should fix the proposed_subsection_html before integration: replace 'tops out at approximately 8,619 kilometers' with either 'tops out at approximately 8,537 km at the disc center, parameterized in WIN-029 as ~9,086 km' (full disclosure of the dome's own internal inconsistency) or simply '~9,086 km' (matches the TLDR and matches the WIN-055 detail_evidence prose). The decay-length number 8,619 km should not appear as a height anywhere.

holes_found.description: The proposed sub_rationale field for the EXP-295 verdict-flip patches is currently audit-trail-only (per the explicit '[audit-trail only -- not rendered]' suffix that the decider has been appending in the four already-integrated WINs -- data/wins.json:WIN-047/048/052/054:sub_rationale all carry that suffix). ISS-1922 tracks the rendering question. This means: when a reader hits WIN-055 on the live site after this final reclassification lands, they will see 'Self-Contradicted' as the verdict label with NO surfaced rationale anywhere in the WIN panel explaining WHY this Misleading-judged-as-textbook-physics WIN was reclassified. The 'See Part 6.13' pointer in the JSON is unrendered. The change-driven curmudgeon process is the audit trail, but it is not the rendered audit trail. From a defender's perspective, the optic is: 'one day the verdict said Misleading, the next day it said Self-Contradicted, with no visible reason'. The 4/5 already-integrated reclassifications carry this same defect, so WIN-055's reclassification does not introduce the defect -- but it propagates it to the final WIN of the package and represents the last opportunity to wire rendering before the entire package is locked in.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should resolve ISS-1922 before integrating EXP-295-P5-WIN-055-verdict, OR explicitly accept the audit-trail-only convention and document it in CLAUDE.md as project policy. Of the three resolution paths the WIN-052.c3 review proposed -- (a) wire sub_rationale rendering, (b) downgrade to audit-trail and document, (c) defer until rendering is wired -- the previously-applied four reclassifications have de-facto chosen path (b) without explicitly closing ISS-1922. Recommend either (i) close ISS-1922 with explicit decision to keep audit-trail-only (and document the convention publicly so Part 6.13 acts as the surfaced rationale), or (ii) take this opportunity to wire rendering before the fifth reclassification lands.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN TLDR inconsistency on bounded-firmament numerical framing. The four already-integrated tldr_verdicts use different bounded-firmament numbers: WIN-047 says 'bounded firmament (max ~30,000 km)' (data/wins.json:WIN-047:tldr_verdict), WIN-052 and WIN-054 say '9,086 km' (data/wins.json:WIN-052,054:tldr_verdict), WIN-048 invokes the copper-firmament chain through WIN-034 instead of a bounded-extent argument. The proposed WIN-055 tldr_verdict uses '9,086 km' -- consistent with WIN-052/054 but inconsistent with WIN-047's '~30,000 km'. The 30,000 km framing in WIN-047 (likely disc radius 20,015 km + firmament height ~9,086 km, or a rounded total spatial-extent claim) is a different concept from the firmament-height-only 9,086 km figure. A skeptical reader who reads multiple WIN tldrs in sequence will notice the inconsistency and may infer either sloppy editing or that the SC claim is fragile under different framings.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional; not blocking for the WIN-055 patch (which is consistent with WIN-052/054). Suggest a separate downstream tightening pass to standardize the bounded-firmament framing across all five reclassified WINs. Best-practice fix: each tldr_verdict cites the specific dome parameter being violated (Schumann-derived firmament height 9,086 km for cosmological-distance WINs; copper-firmament opacity for CMB WINs) rather than a generic 'bounded firmament' number. WIN-052/054/055 use this; WIN-047 currently doesn't and probably should be updated in a future c4 pass.

holes_found.description: The proposed new tldr_verdict for WIN-055 ('Cepheid and SBF distance ladders place galaxies at 10-100 Mpc, orders of magnitude beyond the dome's 9,086 km firmament. The WIN cites them as confirmation while the dome's own commitments deny the spatial framework in which the measurements occur.') drops the existing tldr's explicit 'no redshift mechanism' framing. The current tldr_verdict reads 'Standard candles confirm Hubble's Law; the dome has no redshift mechanism. The values are textbook physics' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:tldr_verdict). The replacement focuses entirely on the spatial-framework / SC argument and leaves the 'standard physics relabeling' critique to the detail_verdict_text. That is defensible -- the SC critique subsumes the relabeling critique on the dome's own terms -- but a reader who only sees the tldr will lose the 'these are textbook standard candles, not dome predictions' point. Same minor concern that WIN-052.c3 hole #3 raised about the false-dilemma drop.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: append a short clause to the new tldr_verdict, e.g. 'The Cepheid period-luminosity relation and SBF magnitude calibration are textbook standard physics anchored to Magellanic parallax (Leavitt 1912, Tonry & Schneider 1988) -- the dome adds nothing.' If kept as drafted, the textbook-physics critique is preserved in detail_verdict_text. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: The 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' sub_rationale label is the LEAST tight fit for WIN-055 of the five-WIN package. WIN-055 covers the Cepheid/SBF distance-ladder, which is not MOND-distinctive: it is the standard cosmological-distance ladder used by everyone, including LambdaCDM cosmologists. Unlike RAR (WIN-052, the canonical MOND showcase) or El Gordo (WIN-054, the Asencio/Banik/Kroupa MOND-paper centerpiece), the Cepheid/SBF ladder is not on McGaugh's Triton Station MOND-anomaly list as a MOND-specific item. It IS on the broader 'tensions LambdaCDM struggles with' list (Hubble tension was specifically the local-vs-CMB-anchor disagreement that arose from these very ladders), so the GROUPING is correct -- this WIN belongs in the operator's 11-WIN bundle. But labeling its sub_rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' overstates the MOND specificity of THIS particular WIN. The MOND-grouping label is the strongest fit for WIN-052; the loosest fit for WIN-055. Same concern WIN-047.c3 raised about Hubble Law not being MOND-distinctive.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: refine sub_rationale wording to 'category-error import (cosmological-tension grouping)' for WIN-047 and WIN-055 specifically (these are Hubble-tension / distance-ladder items rather than MOND-distinctive items), keeping 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' for WIN-052/054/048 (the actual MOND-canon items). This is a labeling refinement, not a verdict-flip blocker. The substantive structural-incoherence argument is identical across all five regardless of sub_rationale wording. If decider prefers single-label simplicity, keeping 'MOND grouping' across all five is acceptable; the Part 6.13 section explicitly groups all eleven WIN-044-054 items as one cross-cutting pattern.

stronger_arguments.description: The standard-candle-ladder framework-independence is a stronger argument than the proposed tldr_verdict currently foregrounds. The WIN-055 detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence) cites THREE independent stellar-physics rungs: Cepheid period-luminosity (Leavitt 1912 + Magellanic Cloud parallax + Gaia DR3), Tip of the Red Giant Branch (TRGB, helium-flash luminosity cutoff, Freedman+ 2019), and Surface Brightness Fluctuations (SBF, Blakeslee+ 2021). All three converge on H_0 = 69.8 +/- 1.9 km/s/Mpc within measurement uncertainty (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence: 'Three independent stellar physics rungs (Cepheids, TRGB, SBF) agree on megaparsec distance scales'). This means a defender CANNOT deflect to 'maybe the cosmological assumptions in your distance ladder are wrong' -- TRGB is anchored to stellar evolution (entirely different physics from Cepheid pulsation), Gaia parallaxes are direct geometric distances. The framework-independence is what makes WIN-055 the strongest SC case against the 'silent extragalactic ontology' counter-argument: the dome cannot avoid the extragalactic measurements by claiming they depend on suspect cosmology.

WIN-055 already explicitly invokes salami-slicing duplication with WIN-047 (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence: 'See also WIN-047, which covers the redshift angle; together they provide independent but converging evidence that the dome has no extragalactic physics -- a salami-slicing duplication (Section 9.1, Pattern #4)'). Reclassifying both WIN-047 (already done qid:318) and WIN-055 (this review) to Self-Contradicted under the same MOND-grouping sub_rationale tightens the salami-slicing observation: the dome filed two SEPARATELY-labeled WINs that differ only in measurement methodology (redshift vs. standard candles) for the same underlying claim (extragalactic distances exist), without internal acknowledgment that either succeeds or fails. The salami-slicing observation now becomes an additional layer on the SC argument -- the dome's claim taxonomy is not just incoherent but double-counts the same incoherence under different methodological labels. This is worth foregrounding in Part 6.13 if not already there.

kernel_of_truth.description: The Cepheid/SBF/TRGB distance ladder is one of the most carefully cross-validated measurement frameworks in observational astrophysics. Henrietta Leavitt's 1912 discovery of the Cepheid period-luminosity relation is the foundational rung; a century of refinement through Hubble, Sandage, the HST Key Project, and the SH0ES collaboration (Riess+ 2022 ApJL 934 L7, 10.3847/2041-8213/ac5c5b) has tightened the calibration to ~1% via Gaia DR3 parallaxes. The Tip of the Red Giant Branch method (Freedman+ 2019 CCHP, ApJ 882 34, 10.3847/1538-4357/ab2f73) gives an independent H_0 measurement using helium-flash luminosity, anchored to entirely different stellar physics from Cepheid pulsation. SBF (Blakeslee+ 2021 ApJ 911 65, 10.3847/1538-4357/abf81c) provides yet another independent rung. The three-rung convergence at H_0 = 69.8 +/- 1.9 km/s/Mpc within measurement uncertainty (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence) is among the most robust empirical results in modern cosmology, and the local-versus-CMB-anchor Hubble tension (the deeper subject of WIN-047) is precisely a tension because BOTH measurements are individually credible. The dome author's instinct to flag distance-ladder/Hubble-tension as a place where standard cosmology is in tension is not unreasonable as a cosmological-tension observation.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The distance-ladder's empirical legitimacy comes precisely from the framework in which it is constructed: Magellanic Cloud parallax at ~50 kpc anchors Cepheid calibration; TRGB anchors at ~few Mpc; SBF reaches 10-100 Mpc; Hubble-flow galaxies extend to several hundred Mpc. Every rung depends on galaxies existing as real 3D objects with measurable internal structure (rotation curves, surface-brightness profiles, stellar populations) at the cited distances (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence). The dome's published parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament height parameterized at 9,086 km via WIN-029, sun ~5,733 km, moon ~2,534 km -- raw-text/05-model.txt) bound the entire spatial framework at order 10^7 m, six orders of magnitude below the smallest scale at which the distance ladder is defined. There is no possible point in the dome's published spatial framework at which the Cepheid period-luminosity relation could be measured, because the dome has no kpc-scale stellar populations in extragalactic systems -- because the dome has no extragalactic systems. Citing the distance-ladder values as confirmation of disc cosmology requires holding two positions simultaneously: galaxies are real measurable distant objects at 10-100 Mpc (so the Cepheid/SBF/TRGB measurements have physical referents), AND the dome's spatial framework is bounded at 30,000 km (so dome cosmology is what its parameters describe). The two cannot both be held -- the SC pattern (2) structure exactly. The kernel-of-truth (the distance ladder is a real empirical achievement worth taking seriously) does not transfer to the dome model because the dome's bounded-firmament commitment forecloses the spatial framework in which the ladder is constructed. NARRATE-CITE: this paragraph cites data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence, raw-text/05-model.txt, and data/wins.json:WIN-029 from this review run.

package_summary_for_decider: ENDORSE the EXP-295 reclassification of WIN-055 from Misleading to Self-Contradicted, completing the five-WIN package (047/048/052/054 already integrated under qid:318-321). WIN-055 is approximately tied with WIN-052 for cleanest existing-prose support: '10-100 Mpc -- orders of magnitude beyond the dome's firmament at 9,086 km' is verbatim in current detail_evidence. Standard-candle framework-independence (Cepheid + TRGB + SBF anchored to Magellanic parallax + Gaia DR3) makes WIN-055 the strongest case against the 'silent extragalactic ontology' counter-argument because the distances are geometric, not redshift-derived. Two moderate findings: (a) the proposed Part 6.13 prose contains a parameter error -- 'tops out at approximately 8,619 kilometers' is wrong (8,619 is the H(r) decay length, not the height; max height is 8,537 km at r=0 or 9,086 km per WIN-029 parameterization); (b) sub_rationale is currently audit-trail-only (ISS-1922 tracks rendering) -- 4/5 reclassifications already in production with this defect, this is the last chance to wire rendering before the package is locked. Three minor findings: (c) cross-WIN tldr_verdict inconsistency on bounded-firmament framing (WIN-047 uses '~30,000 km'; WIN-052/054/055 use '9,086 km'); (d) the new tldr_verdict drops the explicit 'no redshift mechanism / textbook physics' relabeling critique (preserved in detail_verdict_text, defensible); (e) 'MOND grouping' sub_rationale label is the loosest fit for WIN-055 (Cepheid/SBF is not MOND-distinctive; it's general cosmological-tension), suggest cosmological-tension grouping label for WIN-047 + WIN-055 specifically. Two stronger-argument suggestions: foreground three-rung convergence framework-independence; foreground salami-slicing observation now that both WIN-047 and WIN-055 are SC. Decider may proceed with EXP-295-P5-WIN-055-verdict patch as drafted, conditional on the Part 6.13 8,619->9,086/8,537 km fix and resolution of ISS-1922 sub_rationale rendering question (or explicit acceptance of audit-trail-only convention).
deeper_analysis: The other side of the argument: a sympathetic dome defender could mount three counter-arguments against this final SC reclassification: (1) 'Misleading was correct because the WIN's argument structure is a false dilemma, not a structural incoherence -- the dome may be silent on extragalactic ontology'; (2) 'broadening Self-Contradicted to cover framework-level coherence dilutes the verdict's specificity'; (3) 'reclassifying five verdicts in flight looks like goalpost-moving regardless of what audit trail you provide'. The first counter-argument fails for WIN-055 specifically because the standard-candle distance ladder is FRAMEWORK-INDEPENDENT -- TRGB and Gaia parallax provide direct geometric distances anchored to stellar physics, not to cosmological assumptions, so the defender cannot retreat to 'your distance ladder is suspect'. The second counter-argument fails because the analyst's pattern-matching survey of the existing 19 SC WINs (data/wins.json:WIN-002,012,014,029,038,044,045,046,049,050,051,053,056,061,067, plus the four EXP-295 just-integrated 047/048/052/054) shows the SC verdict has been applied for two patterns: numerical/textual conflicts (WIN-038 'cavity cannot produce 7.83 Hz at any radius'; WIN-053 '1,660:1 ring-magnet contradiction'; WIN-067 'Antarctic gravity drop'; WIN-044 'formula version-unstable') AND structural-coherence failures (WIN-029 'Schumann requires hard ceiling', WIN-053 '64,852 nT vs 39 nT'). WIN-055 fits the structural pattern. The third counter-argument is the strongest, but mitigations are real: the existing detail_evidence prose for all five WINs argues SC-tier conclusions verbatim -- the verdict label was lagging the prose, not the prose being rewritten to match a new verdict. The Part 6.13 section provides a single-page audit trail. The change-driven curmudgeon process (this review file is part of) creates an externally-visible record of the reclassification decision sequence. The optic concern remains real but is mitigated; not blocking. From the curmudgeon's adversarial role, the defender's best attack on WIN-055 specifically is NOT the SC reclassification itself (the existing prose makes the case) but the Part 6.13 '8,619 km tops out' parameter error (moderate finding above) and the unrendered sub_rationale (moderate finding above) -- both of which are about the EXECUTION of the package rather than the substantive verdict logic. Net: ENDORSE the WIN-055 reclassification; flag execution concerns for decider attention before package is finalized. WIN-055 is the SECOND-cleanest SC case of the five (after WIN-052), uses standard-candle framework-independence as its load-bearing argument, and represents the appropriate close of the five-WIN package.

The MOND-grouping argument's substantive structure does not depend on the timing detail (the import-vs-convergence distinction holds regardless of whether the imports took 32 minutes or 32 hours); recommend the section deemphasize the timing specifics if challenged, but no action required unless challenged.

curmudgeon_routing_note_responses.item_1_per_WIN_prose_justifies_SC: VERIFIED. WIN-055 detail_evidence verbatim states '10-100 Mpc -- orders of magnitude beyond the dome's firmament at 9,086 km' and 'The dome model has no spatial framework for where these galaxies exist'. This is SC-tier prose; the verdict label was lagging the argument. WIN-052 was spot-checked separately by WIN-052.c3 review (qid:320) and confirmed. WIN-054 was spot-checked by WIN-054.c3 review (qid:321) and confirmed.
curmudgeon_routing_note_responses.item_2_kill_shot_tab_framing_matches: VERIFIED via live fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/killshot.html (2026-05-09 this review run). The tab is structured as six side-by-side discriminating tests pairing dome-and-globe numerical predictions: Sydney-Perth rail (4,352 vs 3,287 km), Polaris elevation 35.9 deg N (36.18 vs 35.91 deg), Aug 12 2026 eclipse magnetic at Ebro (-28.9 vs 0 nT), NMP drift rate by 2027 (<20 km/yr vs no globe prediction), SAA African cell (<21,500 vs ~21,800 nT), JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream (eastbound >5% advantage vs 0%). The killshot-rule footer explicitly says 'A clean result only counts as a major discriminator if the test is genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation.'
